title: Within-race-lap ranking expert
summary: At any given race-lap only a few drivers are realistic next-stop candidates, so ranking drivers against same-lap peers can capture pit waves better than pure independent classification.
rationale: At any given race-lap only a few drivers are realistic next-stop candidates, so ranking drivers against same-lap peers can capture pit waves better than pure independent classification. Official ranking objectives in CatBoost and LightGBM are designed exactly for grouped comparisons.
implementation_hint: Build a specialist with group_id = Race-Year-LapNumber and relevance = PitNextLap. Train QuerySoftMax/YetiRank or LambdaRank on features that are comparable within the lap: tyre-age percentile, degradation percentile, position, delta-to-race-lap median, stint number, and stop-debt flags. Blend its scores with the base classifier OOF.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering among simultaneous candidates and sharpen probabilities on clustered stop laps.
risk: Query groups with zero or one positive are common, so ranking may be unstable unless blended; group leakage occurs if race-level normalizations use held-out laps.
